repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3: ThmSmith/PartialID empty at seed time; closest panel catalogue is FWL/regression-weight material, not SC partial-ID support intervals

published_prior_art.cite: NazaretShiBlei2024 gives SC misspecification-error bounds; FermanPinto2021 studies imperfect pre-fit estimator behavior, but neither states this signed-vector Chebyshev projection interval
AbadieDiamondHainmueller2010: exact-fit linear-factor synthetic-control point-identification corner

repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3 PartialID empty; q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends concerns aggregate DID pretrend summaries, not synthetic-control RMSPE/factor-bridge anisotropy

published_prior_art.cite: FirpoPossebom2018 uses RMSPE for sharp-null/placebo sensitivity reports and CattaneoFengTitiunik2021 gives prediction intervals; neither appears to subsume deterministic q-scalar nonsharpness
Generic partial-identification/support-function logic is classical; no verified SC paper identifies H(P) as the minimal deterministic misspecification diagnostic

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The sharpness-failure target names RMSPE/prediction-interval workflows, but no quoted published claim uses scalar q as a sharp deterministic factor-misspecification state; current tier=letter below novelty_target=field unless tied to a published scalar-calibration rule or estimator workflow.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: Exact-factor point identification is already the ADH 2010 corner; keep it as a sanity reduction, not a main novelty result.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Attach Conjecture 1 to a specific published scalar-calibration or robustness table rule that actually treats RMSPE/MSPE as the sensitivity state, with the exact quoted claim and bibkey.
Promote the anisotropic witness to an explicit open-set theorem with a quantitative gap lower bound in terms of |H_+(g1)-H_+(g2)|, so the result is not just a two-point demonstration.
Move Theorem 2 out of §8 Main results or relabel it as a sanity corollary of Theorem 1, since it is knowingly ADH prior art.
Add one comparison to the general partial-identification/support-function literature explaining why this is more than a direct Manski-style projection specialization.

recommended_next_step: Revise the novelty target for Conjecture 1 by naming and quoting a published scalar RMSPE/MSPE calibration workflow, or pivot the proposal toward the positive Chebyshev interval as a deterministic reporting theorem.
